column2: A small business needs Windows systems that can join Active Directory, use BitLocker, and host Remote Desktop sessions. Which Windows edition tier is the better fit: Home or Pro?
column5: Home editions are consumer-focused. Pro adds the business management and security features named in the scenario.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.3; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, pp. 5-6
column10: A+::220-1202::1.3 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsEditions Basic HighYield Windows Editions Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which Windows 10 edition is aimed at high-end desktops that need enhanced performance, high RAM support, more physical CPUs, and ReFS support?
column5: Do not confuse this with Enterprise. Pro for Workstations is the workstation-class Windows 10 edition, while Enterprise is aimed at large organization deployment and management.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.3; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 5
column10: A+::220-1202::1.3 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsEditions Basic Windows Editions Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which Windows edition tier is designed for large company deployments with volume licensing and advanced centralized management?
column5: Enterprise is the large-organization tier. Messer's examples include volume licensing and advanced management capabilities rather than home-user customization.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.3; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, pp. 5-6
column10: A+::220-1202::1.3 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsEditions Basic HighYield Windows Editions Enterprise Source::Messer-v140

column2: A European Windows system is missing Windows Media Player and other multimedia utilities by design. Which Windows edition variant does this describe?
column5: N editions are not a separate management tier. They are Windows editions with media components removed; the Media Feature Pack can add the missing media features later.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.3; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 6
column10: A+::220-1202::1.3 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsEditions Basic Windows Editions Media Source::Messer-v140

column2: What is the key difference between a Windows workgroup and a Windows domain?
column4: A workgroup is a peer-style set of standalone computers, while a domain uses centralized authentication and device access for a business network.
column5: The exam distinction is management scope. Workgroups keep control local to each device; domains centralize identity and access for many devices.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.3; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 6
column10: A+::220-1202::1.3 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsEditions Basic HighYield Windows Domains Source::Messer-v140

column2: A user has Windows Home, but the business needs the computer joined to an Active Directory domain. What edition issue should the technician recognize?
column4: Windows Home is not the right edition for domain joining; the system needs Pro or a higher business edition.
column5: This is a common edition trap. Domain support is a business feature associated with Pro and Enterprise, not Home.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.3; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, pp. 5-6
column10: A+::220-1202::1.3 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsEditions Basic HighYield Windows Domains Editions Source::Messer-v140

column2: How does a managed work desktop usually differ from a home Windows desktop?
column4: A managed work desktop is usually standardized with limited customization, while a home desktop allows broader personal customization.
column5: The objective's "desktop styles/user interface" item is about recognizing the support and management difference, not memorizing a theme or wallpaper setting.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.3; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 6
column10: A+::220-1202::1.3 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsEditions Basic Windows UI Source::Messer-v140

column2: A technician needs a Windows 11 computer to accept inbound Remote Desktop connections. Which edition limitation matters?
column4: The Remote Desktop service is available in Pro and Enterprise editions, not Home.
column5: RDP clients are broadly available, but hosting an inbound Remote Desktop session is the edition-dependent feature.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.3; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 6
column10: A+::220-1202::1.3 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsEditions Basic HighYield Windows RDP Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which Windows feature provides full-disk encryption for the operating system drive?
column5: BitLocker protects the whole drive. EFS is different because it protects individual files and folders on NTFS.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.3; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, pp. 5-6
column10: A+::220-1202::1.3 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsEditions Basic HighYield Windows Encryption Source::Messer-v140

column2: What is gpedit.msc used for on a Windows computer?
column4: It opens the Local Group Policy Editor to manage local system and user policy settings.
column5: Local Group Policy applies to one device. In Active Directory environments, Group Policy can also be centrally managed through domain tooling.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.3; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 6
column10: A+::220-1202::1.3 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsEditions Basic Windows GroupPolicy Source::Messer-v140

column2: Before deploying Windows 11, which two hardware capabilities from Objective 1.3 should a technician verify?
column5: Messer specifically notes TPM 2.0 compatibility and hardware capable of Secure Boot through UEFI. The official objective calls out TPM and UEFI as the hardware-requirement comparison points.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.3; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 5
column10: A+::220-1202::1.3 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsEditions Basic HighYield Windows Upgrade Hardware Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which upgrade path should be chosen when the goal is to keep supported applications, documents, and settings in place?
column5: A clean install starts over and requires backup and application reinstall planning. In-place upgrade is the preservation-focused path when compatibility allows it.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.3; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 4
column10: A+::220-1202::1.3 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsEditions Basic HighYield Windows Upgrade Source::Messer-v140

column2: Why is a 32-bit-only CPU a blocker for a Windows 11 deployment?
column5: This is a hardware-compatibility distinction between older systems and Windows 11 deployment planning.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.3; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 6
column10: A+::220-1202::1.3 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsEditions Basic Windows Hardware Source::Messer-v140

column2: How does Windows 11 Home's Device Encryption differ from BitLocker availability in Windows 11 Pro?
column4: Windows 11 Home includes consumer-oriented Device Encryption, while Windows 11 Pro provides BitLocker full-disk encryption.
column5: This card is about edition comparison. The official objective names BitLocker, and Messer distinguishes Windows 11 Home Device Encryption from Pro's BitLocker feature.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.3; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 6
column10: A+::220-1202::1.3 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsEditions Basic Windows Encryption Editions Source::Messer-v140

column2: What should a technician remember about RAM support when comparing Windows editions?
column4: RAM support varies by edition; more advanced editions can support more RAM.
column5: For example, Messer identifies Windows 10 Pro for Workstations as a high-maximum-RAM edition. Avoid assuming every edition supports the same memory ceiling.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.3; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, pp. 5-6
column10: A+::220-1202::1.3 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsEditions Basic Windows Hardware Editions Source::Messer-v140

column2: When is a clean install the better upgrade path than an in-place upgrade?
column4: Use a clean install when the goal is to start fresh instead of preserving the existing applications, files, and settings.
column5: This is the opposite side of the in-place upgrade decision. Clean installs require data backup and application reinstall planning because the old environment is not preserved.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.3; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 4
column10: A+::220-1202::1.3 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsEditions Basic Windows Upgrade Source::Messer-v140
